title: Paepalanthus geniculatus

This plant belongs to the genus Paepalanthus, which is a large and diverse group of plants native to the Americas. The Eriocaulaceae family is further divided into several genera, each with distinct characteristics and habitats. Paepalanthus geniculatus is a member of the Eriocaulales order, a group of monocotyledonous plants that are often found in aquatic or wetland environments. The plant's classification is an important tool for understanding its evolutionary history and relationships to other species within the family.
